title: China: Inflation and trade data support modest recovery – ING
snippet: ING economists Lynn Song and Min Joo Kang expect China’s February CPI inflation to pick up to 1.0% year-on-year, mainly due to Lunar New Year effects, while the impact of higher Oil prices should appear later.

title: CNY: Flows diverge as safe haven doubts grow – BNY
snippet: BNY’s Head of Markets Macro Strategy Bob Savage notes a sharp divergence between CNY forwards and spot, suggesting hedge unwinding alongside asset outflows. CNY has outperformed peers, yet the bank questions its safe haven role as spot flows show large outflows tied to expatriation.

title: China: Inflation and trade data signal firmer cycle – ABN AMRO
snippet: ABN AMRO expects China’s CPI inflation to rebound to around 1% year-on-year in February, driven by Lunar New Year spending and base effects after a 0.2% reading in January.

The GDPNow model estimate for real GDP growth (seasonally adjusted annual rate) in the first quarter of 2026 is 2.1 percent on March 6, down from 3.0 percent on March 2. title: USD/CNH: Upside risks as strong fix meets USD demand – OCBC
snippet: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note that USD/CNH has traded higher as Iran-related geopolitical tensions support the Dollar. Beijing has been setting a stronger CNY fix, which has helped stabilise the Renminbi and partially offset broader Asian FX softness.

title: USD/CHF Price Forecast: Struggles at 0.7800, dives below 50-day SMA
snippet: The USD/CHF pair retreats on Friday during the North American session, down 0.53% as the Greenback weakens following a dismal jobs report, and a break to four day low beneath the 0.7800 figure.

title: Fed’s Hammack: Inflation problem is broader than just tariffs
snippet: Beth Hammack, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Cleveland, said that inflation is too high, adding that inflationary pressures are broad-based at the United States (US) Monetary Policy Forum in New York City on Friday.

title: USD/MYR: Consolidation near highs with upside risks – OCBC
snippet: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong report that USD/MYR is consolidating near recent highs after an early-week run-up, supported by broader USD strength and soggy risk sentiment. Geopolitical headlines around Iran and energy markets are seen as key drivers.

title: Forecasting the upcoming week: Middle East war boosts Oil, NFP miss shakes US Dollar
snippet: The Middle East crisis has escalated into an all-out war after the US and Israel assassinated the Supreme Leader of Iran on February 28. Iran not only targeted Israel but also attacked United States (US) military bases around the Persian Gulf.

title: Silver Price Forecast: XAG/USD range-bound as RSI holds near 50 and MACD flattens
snippet: Silver (XAG/USD) trades modestly higher on Friday as the US Dollar (USD) and Treasury yields ease following softer-than-expected US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data. Despite the intraday bounce, the white metal remains on track for its first weekly decline in three weeks.

title: Fed’s Collins: Fed policy is well positioned
snippet: Susan Collins, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Boston, said that to cut interest rates again, the Fed needs to see clear evidence of inflation ebbing, adding that she sees no need to change the monetary policy stance urgently at a gathering in Springfield, Massachusetts on Friday.

title: Crude Oil explodes higher as Strait of Hormuz crisis deepens
snippet: WTI crude oil surged about 11% on Friday, breaching above $87.00, its highest level since October 2023, in a session dominated by a single massive bullish candle that dwarfed every session of the past three months.

title: NZD/USD Price Forecast: Hold above 200-day SMA, Kiwi approaches 0.5900
snippet: The New Zealand Dollar erases some of its earlier losses, remains above the 200-day Simple Moving Average (SMA) of 0.5874, seems poised to finish Friday’s session virtually unchanged.

title: Fed’s Miran: Fed typically does not respond to oil prices
snippet: Stephen Miran, Federal Reserve (Fed) governor said that he is hesitant to read too much into one month’s job report and that policy, adding that if anything, it biases him towards an even more dovish policy in an interview with CNBC on Friday.

title: USD/CAD dips as weak US NFP data and rising Oil prices strengthen Loonie
snippet: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) strengthens against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday as the Greenback loses momentum following softer-than-expected US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data.

title: Gold price surges after weak US payrolls shake US Dollar
snippet: Gold price (XAU/USD) rallies on Friday during the North American session, boosted by a weaker-than-expected US jobs report and a risk-off market mood amid the Middle East conflict. At the time of writing, XAU/USD trades near $5,140, up more than 1%.

title: USD/CHF dips on US job contraction, geopolitical tensions
snippet: USD/CHF declines on Friday, trading around 0.7780 at the time of writing, down 0.44% on the day, as the Swiss Franc (CHF) benefits from increased safe-haven demand amid economic and geopolitical uncertainty.

title: AUD/USD advances on US employment contraction, RBA rate hike outlook
snippet: AUD/USD trades around 0.7020 on Friday at the time of writing, up 0.11% on the day, as investors digest a much weaker-than-expected US employment report.

title: Gold steadies after soft US jobs data but heads for weekly loss
snippet: Gold (XAU/USD) gains traction on Friday after trading under pressure for most of the day, with the US Dollar (USD) and Treasury yields easing as traders digest soft US Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) data.

title: USD/JPY poised to test 158.00 despite weak US jobs data
snippet: USD/JPY rises and challenges the 158.00 figure on Friday, up over 0.20% after the latest US employment report signaled weakness in the labor market. Also, the Middle East conflict deteriorates market mood, maintaining the US Dollar (USD) bid during the week.

title: USD: Fed cuts repriced on higher inflation – ING
snippet: ING’s James Knightley notes that expectations for Federal Reserve easing in 2026 have been reduced as higher near-term US inflation and resilient growth make early rate cuts less likely.

title: GBP/USD trades flat despite soft US jobs data
snippet: The British Pound (GBP) struggles for direction against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday, with GBP/USD consolidating after a short-lived spike following weaker-than-expected US labor market data. At the time of writing, the pair is trading around 1.3362, on course for a third straight weekly decline.

title: Fed: Wait-and-see stance after weak jobs – Commerzbank
snippet: Commerzbank Senior Economist Dr. Christoph Balz notes that US employment fell by 92,000 in February, far below expectations, with revisions also lowering prior months. He highlights possible distortions from a strike and cold weather.

title: Fed’s Daly: Both goals are risks now
snippet: Mary Daly, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of San Francisco, said that a month of data isn’t decisive, adding that inflation is above target right now, balancing risks of calculation at an interview with CNBC on Friday.

title: ‘I find this very worrying’: A friend’s home fell into foreclosure. What on earth is going on?
snippet: Federal Housing Authority and Veterans Affairs home loans are seeing higher delinquency rates than conventional loans.

title: Rupee gains 2 paise to 91.62 against US dollar in early trade
snippet: At the interbank foreign exchange market, the rupee opened at its previous session's closing level of 91.64 and gained 2 paise to 91.62 against the greenback

title: Forex Today: US Dollar firms ahead of NFP, Oil surges amid Middle East war
snippet: The US Dollar (USD) is being supported by crude oil prices, which rose to its highest level since July 2024, amid headlines of potential interruptions to the Strait of Hormuz and attacks on vessels in the region.

title: Rupee makes smart recovery from previous days lows
snippet: The Rupee recovers from record lows amid geopolitical tensions, closing stronger against the Dollar despite ongoing pressure from oil prices.

title: UAE mulls freezing Iranian assets as Middle East conflict escalates: WSJ
snippet: The UAE is considering freezing billions of dollars of Iranian assets held in the Gulf state, potentially severing one of Tehran's most critical economic lifelines.

snippet: Weak jobs report, higher gas prices move central bank to the sidelines.

title: ECB’s Schnabel: Iran war creates upside inflation risks
snippet: Isabel Schnabel, European Central Bank (ECB) member, said that the ECB’s is still in a good place even though the Iran war created some upside inflation risks at the 2026 United States (US) Monetary Policy Forum in New York on Friday.
